Otari by Mozilla AI

Otari by Mozilla AIControl Your LLM Infrastructure [...] Ben Senescu

Hello 👋   
  
This February, I was building other open source projects and wanted to do SEO.   
  
I was floored by how expensive and bloated the tools were. There weren't any modern open source solutions, so I figured I should just build that.  
  
Since then, it's been really cool seeing founders and agencies enjoy the product and even building their own custom features.   
  
Hope you give it a try: openseo.so. There are some trial credits to test it out, then it starts at $10/month.   
  
You can also self host on Cloudflare or with Docker.   
  
Give us a star on github or you can join the discord to get SEO advice, contribute, or just hang out.

Report

Hayriye [...] Report

Hayriye

Love how clean the MCP integration setup is. Most SEO tools bury that kind of connection behind ten menus, but here it feels like a one-click setup that actually respects how someone using an AI agent wants to work.

results.title: Turn off the Eiffel Tower
results.content:
## Ahrefs has an open source problem

Ben Senescu (@bensenescu) wanted his open source app to rank on Google, found every serious SEO tool starts north of $100 a month, and built OpenSEO in days: keyword research, rank tracking, backlinks and site audits, open source and self-hostable, or hosted from $10.

🔥 Our Take: Ahrefs and Semrush charge what they charge because they crawl their own indexes. Ben skips that entirely: the data comes from DataForSEO pay-as-you-go, and the product is the workflow around it. The reason this lands now is agents. Half the launch thread is people wiring it into Claude over MCP, and as one commenter put it, an agent with no data sounds exactly as certain as one with it. Ten dollars buys the difference.

## Your canvas just learned to design [...] newsletter icon

July 19th, 2026

# Turn off the Eiffel Tower

## Turn off the Eiffel Tower

gm legends, happy Sunday.

Today: a world map with a filter that deletes famous places, open source comes for your $300 SEO bill, and a duck that knows that discount is fake.

## This map hides famous places

Alban (@flightmussy), whose whole Product Hunt bio is "creating stuff for fun," built Detourmap: one interactive map of 70,000 places pulled from Wikidata, from waterfalls and catacombs to 584 ghost towns, each ranked on a fame scale that runs from world-famous down to hidden gem. Free, no account, no ads. [...] 🔥 Our Take: Google Maps knows every one of these places and will never show them to you, because it ranks by popularity and popularity is a loop: famous places get the pins, the pins keep them famous. Detourmap's fame filter breaks the loop. Narrow it to hidden gems and the Eiffel Tower vanishes while a Soviet mining town on Svalbard stays. One commenter lost twenty minutes wandering Romanian castles. The whole dataset is downloadable and public domain. This is the good kind of internet.

## Ahrefs has an open source problem
